The '''Mark VII "Inquisitor" Series Seeker Droid ''' was a [[Seeker (droid)/Legends|seeker]] developed by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]]. It was used to hunt down so-called "enemies of the state."

==Description==
The Mark VII Inquisitors were small and spherical [[Seeker (droid)/Legends|seekers]] manufactured by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]]. The seeker floated through the air, powered by a small [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] generator that gave it a top speed of forty [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] and a flight ceiling of fifty [[Meter/Legends|meters]].<ref name="EGWT" /> Some variaties were similar to [[Marksman-H combat remote|Marksman H Combat Remotes]] in both form and function, such as the type used by [[Kyle Katarn]] during the [[Empire Reborn campaign|Empire Reborn Campaign]].<ref name="Outcast">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''</ref>

The Mark VII was equipped with an Arakyd [[BXT-4 central processing computer]], paired with an [[AA-1 VerboBrain/Legends|AA-1 VerboBrain]]. The VerboBrain allowed the seeker to understand fifty thousand [[Language/Legends|languages]], which its [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]] could replicate, including organic languages and the electronic droid languages. The Mark VII possessed a high level of [[Artificial intelligence/Legends|artificial intelligence]].<ref name="EGWT" />

A sophisticated [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] suite was installed in the Mark VII; four [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] were spaced evenly around the lower hemisphere, giving the seeker three-hundred-and-sixty degree vision. Integrated [[Electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]] allowed the Mark VII to zoom in on objects up to two hundred meters distant. Directional [[Audio sensor/Legends|audio sensors]] could hear in frequencies both above and below the limits of [[Human/Legends|Human]] hearing, and could record any conversation within a hundred meters of the seeker. An internal [[communications transceiver]] could monitor all local [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] frequencies. [[Heat sensor]]s with [[infrared scope]]s were used to track heat sources and locate footprints. The Mark VII also made use of a [[genetic sampler]] that could take samples of [[Hair/Legends|hair]], [[Blood/Legends|blood]], and skin. These samples would be compared to internal records to determine their source.<ref name="EGWT" />

The seeker was also fitted with a [[data probe]] that allowed the Mark VII to interface with other systems, such as [[Computer/Legends|computers]], security systems, and communications equipment. The seeker would then be able to review and download relevant information and files. One of the Mark VII's preferred tactic was to override droid controls and compel them to share their information.<ref name="EGWT" />

The Mark VII had a variety of mission profiles, including capture, interrogation, or elimination. While tracking its target, the Mark VII preferred to stay out sight, recording conversations as evidence and working to confirm the identity of the target. Once the target's identity was confirmed, the seeker would carry out its assigned task. If the mission was to eliminate the target, it would engage with weapons; in the case of capture or interrogation, the Mark VII would force its prey to a safe location at gunpoint.<ref name="AE" />

To complete this goal, the Mark VII was equipped with a [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]], and armed with two [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] that were as powerful as a standard [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]]. The blasters could kill or stun, and had a maximum range of fifty meters, with an optimal range of twenty-five. The seeker's [[Power cell/Legends|power cells]] held enough energy to fire one hundred shots.<ref name="EGWT" />

==History==
Mark VII Inquisitors were used extensively by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Although they were eventually outlawed by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], criminal organizations and Imperial-sympathizer governments still used continued to use them.<ref name="EGWT" />
